purpose: Single source of truth for option-P&L label generation. Both the offline labeler AND the runtime strategy engine MUST honor every clause below. Divergence between training-time labels and runtime execution silently destroys model edge — this contract makes the equivalence checkable.

atm_selection.rule: Read snapshot.atm_strike directly. Do NOT recompute ATM at label time — the snapshot already has the canonical value the runtime will see.
atm_selection.divergence_failure: Different ATM than runtime → label refers to a contract the runtime would never trade.

expiry_selection.rule: Use the expiry the snapshot ingested with the chain. We do NOT support roll logic in label v1 — if the chain's expiry is wrong, the snapshot is wrong upstream of us.
expiry_selection.divergence_failure: Label uses next-week expiry while runtime trades this-week → completely different theta + IV.

strike_step.rule: Used only for OTM/ITM recipes. For ATM recipes the strike comes directly from snapshot.atm_strike; for OTM/ITM the labeler must add N * strike_step. Runtime smart-strike selector uses the SAME snapshot.strike_step() call.

entry_price.rule: Use close-of-minute(t0) for the selected contract from the options parquet.
entry_price.runtime_equivalent: strategy_app entry-fill at snap.option_ltp(direction, strike) — see strategy_app/engines/trade_signal_builder.py:41. snap.option_ltp returns the chain row's ce_ltp/pe_ltp, which corresponds to bar close in historical replay.

exit_price.rule: Use close-of-minute(t0 + offset) for the SAME contract chosen at t0. Strike is frozen at decision time. Compare close to stop_premium and target_premium at each bar.
exit_price.runtime_equivalent: strategy_app/position/tracker.py:_is_stop_hit (line 180), _check_premium_target_hit, and HARD_CLOSE_MINUTE/SOFT_CLOSE_MINUTE checks (line 97, 115). Stops checked on close only (snap.option_ltp), NEVER on intra-bar OHLC.
exit_price.audit_note_2026_05_17: Confirmed: runtime checks current_premium = snap.option_ltp(...) once per snapshot. Labeler MUST check close only — using bar.high/bar.low would create train-runtime mismatch (labels see optimistic intra-bar fills the runtime can't achieve).

cost_model.rule: The labeler MUST import and call TradingCostModel().breakdown(entry_value, exit_value) — do not re-derive a 200-bps-style flat. The canonical class already handles brokerage (Rs.40 round-trip flat) + charges (5 bps round-trip on notional) + slippage (15 bps round-trip on notional).
cost_model.effective_round_trip_on_low_premium_opti: For a premium of Rs.100 x 15 lot size = Rs.1500 entry value, total round-trip cost is ~Rs.43 (Rs.40 brokerage + Rs.3 charges/slippage) = ~2.87% of premium. Cost-as-%-of-premium varies inversely with premium size.
cost_model.rationale: Reusing the canonical cost class keeps labeler and runtime aligned by construction. B1's 200-bps-flat was correct for futures-notional labels but wrong for option-premium labels — same brokerage in absolute Rs becomes a much bigger fraction of a Rs.100 option than of a Rs.50000 futures contract.
cost_model.runtime_equivalent: strategy_app live + replay fill paths must use the same TradingCostModel for net-pnl computation. MemorySignalLogger already does (see test_research_cost_model.py).

risk_basis.rule: All option-P&L-trained recipes MUST set risk_basis=option_premium in their stage3 recipe metadata. Stops/targets are in % of premium, not % of underlying.
risk_basis.guard: runtime_contract.py:57-61 rejects option_premium recipes with stops < 1% — keep that guard in place.

liquidity_filter.rationale: A contract with <1000 OI or <Rs.5 premium is untradeable; labeling such rows pollutes the training set with fills the runtime would never get.

session_boundaries.rule: Labeler MUST import these constants directly from strategy_app.constants, not duplicate the values. If session timing or lot size changes, both labeler and runtime pick up the update atomically.
session_boundaries.no_exits_after: 15:15 IST (hard close — even an open position is force-closed)

label_definition.comment: v1 uses simple sign-of-net-pnl as the binary label. After-cost positive => 1. This matches B1 cost-aware semantics. Higher thresholds (e.g. >2%) can be tried in v2 once the v1 signal is verified.

sanity_report_gates.rationale: Pre-training sanity. If labels fall outside these bands, the labeler has a bug or the data has a coverage gap — do NOT proceed to training.
